items.title: Behind Khamenei’s funeral, Iran’s bid to show strength
items.description: Ahou Koutchesfahani, scholar in international relations and Iran expert, analyses the funeral ceremonies for Ali Khamenei as a carefully orchestrated display of power by the Islamic Republic. The regime is seeking to send two messages: to its own population, by showcasing its control and ability to mobilise large crowds, and to the international community, by reinforcing the image of Khamenei as an anti-imperialist figure who stood up to the United States. Through these ceremonies, Tehran is also attempting to strengthen its narrative and rally support beyond its borders.

items.title: Notes from a NATO Summit: An Estonian Perspective
items.description: As the week draws to a close following the NATO summit in Ankara, a clearer picture is emerging of what was happening behind closed doors inside the Presidential Compound as the alliance's 32 leaders met. Among the revelations, reports from The Wall Street Journal and The Times of Israel suggest President Trump's team was rapidly adjusting plans amid what appeared to be an ongoing Iranian assassination threat. Away from the main sessions, the summit was also shaped by a series of side meetings, including talks between the Baltic leaders and President Zelensky, while President Erdogan's controversial gifts to visiting leaders – ceremonial pistols presented with ammunition enclosed – prompted an unexpected diplomatic and ethical dilemma over whether to accept them. France 24's Gavin Lee spoke to Joakim Klementi, European correspondent for Estonian Public Broadcasting, ERR.

items.description: Pirates have captured our imagination for centuries. With "Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced," gamers can now head off on another pirate adventure. How has our concept of pirates evolved with pop culture?

items.title: Attacks on Kyiv ahead of NATO summit: What is Putin's goal?
items.description: Kyiv suffered heavy Russian strikes ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara. Experts say Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to send a message.

items.title: Port Arthur: The shooting that changed Australia
items.description: Thirty years ago, Australia experienced the deadliest mass shooting in its history. The Port Arthur massacre transformed the country’s relationship to guns and reshaped firearms policy for decades. Now, following the Bondi Beach attack in December 2025, Australia is once again questioning whether those reforms are still sufficient. Mairead Dundas revisits Port Arthur for France 24

items.title: Trump flags more US-Iran talks but maintains ceasefire is 'over'
items.description: Qatari negotiators meet officials in Iran to seek to de-escalate tensions as Donald Trump says the US has agreed to Iran's request to continue talks.

items.title: EU tells Instagram, Facebook to change addictive features or risk fines
Argentina's gruelling World Cup schedule a concern for Scaloni
items.description: Leandro Paredes, Nicolás Tagliafico and Julián Álvarez set to return to starting line-up for clash against Egypt. Leer más

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Nolan Wells' family demands answers about US teen's death after boating trip
items.description: Wells' body was found in the water near Mississippi's Horn Island days after he went on a boating trip there with friends.

items.title: Anambra approves three-month tax waiver for business owners
items.description: The Commissioner for Information and Value Reformation, Law Mefor, disclosed this in a statement on Thursday in Awka. The post Anambra approves three-month tax waiver for business owners appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Paris court hands monitor 18-month suspended sentence for assaulting preschooler in abuse scandal
items.description: A Paris court handed a school monitor an 18-month suspended prison sentence on Friday for sexually abusing a kindergarten-age child. It's the first such guilty verdict since the French capital was rocked by a wave of allegations of child sex abuse by non-teaching staff in Paris's public school system.

items.title: UPDATED: Kidnapped Oyo pupils, teachers freed
items.description: The release followed months of operations involving security forces from the Office of the National Security Adviser (ONSA), the State Security Service (SSS), the Nigerian Army and other security agencies. The post UPDATED: Kidnapped Oyo pupils, teachers freed appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: West Asia war highlights: Former Iranian supreme leader buried, says Iranian state TV
items.description: Donald Trump briefed Benjamin Netanyahu about U.S. operations in the Gulf, the Israeli Prime Minister’s office said

items.title: France ends Morocco’s run at World Cup 2026
items.description: Morocco’s historic World Cup 2026 journey came to an end as France secured a 2–0 victory in the quarter-finals, bringing the African team’s impressive campaign to a close.

items.title: Milei’s allied governors open door to suspension of PASO primaries
items.description: Government is seeking support to advance with its electoral reform push ahead of next voting cycle; Senate to have final say on potential suspension of compulsory primaries. Leer más

items.title: Watch: India and Australia Deepen Defence Ties: What It Means for the Indo-Pacific | Above the Fold | 09.07.2026
items.description: From Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Australia to the escalating conflict in West Asia, to new developments in the Sri Lanka prison clashes, we bring you the latest in this episode. We also look at the Navy’s Drishti-10 UAV that crashed near Gujarat, the controversy surrounding Argentina’s latest FIFA World Cup win, and other top national and international stories. We bring you your complete news roundup.

items.title: Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei laid to rest months after being killed in Iran war
items.description: Khamenei’s flag-covered coffin was carried aloft into the shrine of Imam Reza in his home city of Mashhad in eastern Iran as a sea of people waited outside and listened to prayers

items.title: New UN report highlights further 'markers of genocide' by warring parties in Sudan
items.description: The United Nations says a new report by it’s Fact-Finding Mission for Sudan provides further evidence that atrocities committed by warring parties in the region constitute markers of genocide

items.title: Argentina targets investment grade rating by 2031, Caputo says
items.description: Milei administration is targeting investment-grade status for Argentina by the end of a potential second term in 2031, announces minister. Leer más

items.title: Argentina shuns markets for now, sees investment grade by 2031
items.description: President Javier Milei’s administration is targeting investment-grade status for Argentina by the end of a potential second term in 2031, announces Economy Minister Luis Caputo. Leer más

items.title: Pies win a thriller against Kangaroos as veteran faces MRO scrutiny
items.description: Collingwood wins a thriller against North Melbourne after a last ditch kick from the Roos hits the turf.

items.title: 8 men indicted in planned drone and sniper attack on White House UFC cage-fighting show
items.description: Eight men were indicted on murder and terrorism conspiracy charges Thursday for their alleged roles in a thwarted drone and sniper attack on the UFC cage-fighting show staged at the White House in June

items.title: What's behind Germany's World Cup woes?
items.description: Germany have endured three straight disappointing World Cups. Experts say the reasons why are connected to football, but also to German society.

items.title: Argentina produce epic World Cup fightback to beat Egypt, reach quarters
items.description: Lionel Messi inspired Argentina to a stunning late comeback as the reigning champions beat Egypt 3-2 on Tuesday to reach the World Cup quarter-finals; Match swung by controversial VAR call against African side. Leer más

items.title: Wildfire in southern Spain leaves at least 12 dead and 23 missing
items.description: A local official said four of the victims may be British after a fire broke out near Los Gallardos, Almería.

items.title: Milei leans on the caste to seek re-election
items.description: The arrival of Diego Santilli as Cabinet Chief signals a pragmatism that is both the result of a lack of trustworthy people in the ranks and Milei’s acknowledgment that he needs yet another dose of “caste” to align the means with the goals of his administration. Leer más

items.title: 'Not gonna talk about that': Luai dodges press conference after another Tigers loss
items.description: The Tigers lose 32-6 to the Warriors at Campbelltown Stadium, with Jarome Luai skipping the post-match press conference after a week of reports about his future with the team.

items.title: U.S.-Iran fighting appears to pause. And, life inside Israel's military zones in Gaza
items.description: Fighting between the U.S. and Iran appears to have paused after two days of clashing amid a shaky ceasefire. And, a look at what life is like inside Israel's expanding zone of control in Gaza.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Syria unveils archaeological treasures returned from France after 15 years
items.description: When French President Emmanuel Macron visited Syria earlier this week, he brought with him a number of archaeological treasures that had been held in France since 2011.

items.title: Prambanan Temple: Why is India restoring Indonesia’s largest Shiva temple? | Explained
items.description: From Angkor Wat to Vat Phou temple, India’s efforts to restore Indonesia’s largest Hindu temple is part of its cultural diplomacy

items.title: Why are politicians and sanctioned states embracing crypto?
items.description: Bitcoin is falling, yet cryptocurrency is spreading beyond investing into politics, sanctions and global finance.

items.title: The United States turns 250
items.description: Much of the population thinks the United States is heading in the wrong direction, but there is no agreement at all on what would be the right one. Leer más

items.title: Sudan: El-Obeid civilians trapped under RSF siege
items.description: The UN has raised the alarm over a worsening humanitarian crisis in El-Obeid, a city besieged by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) in Sudan, where many civilians are dying amid relentless attacks. FRANCE 24 correspondent Bastien Renoul spoke to residents trapped inside, who describe bombed water infrastructure, constant strikes and growing fears of famine as food supplies run out.

items.title: Milei urges governors to renew commitment to 'May Pact' in speech
items.description: President says Argentina is entering a "Second Independence" as he outlines new reform agenda and praises his "cabinet of patriots." Leer más

items.title: Cardi B, Teyana Taylor and Bad Bunny brave Paris heat
items.description: Paris has been hosting its autumn-winter couture collections amid simmering temperatures, with celebrities sporting parasols and fans as they watch the shows. Fashion journalist Alice Pfeiffer tells us why "Blazymania" has taken hold, as Chanel's new artistic director continues his winning streak with designs inspired by Coco Chanel herself. At Dior, Jonathan Anderson presents an accessible, floral collection, while Pierpaolo Piccioli draws stars including Cynthia Erivo, Demi Moore and Teyana Taylor to the front row for his Balenciaga debut.

items.title: The search for Gaza’s missing brings heartbreak and closure for loved ones
items.description: Thousands of bodies remain trapped under the rubble of their homes in Gaza, with retrieval a long and difficult task.

items.title: 'My childhood passion is now a lifetime commitment': Wildlife conservationist Krithi Karanth
items.description: One of the world's leading, multi-award-winning environmentalists has told FRANCE 24 how a childhood passion became a lifelong commitment. Krithi Karanth explained how her father nurtured her love of nature over 17 years, taking her into the jungle from the age of just one. She is now a conservation biologist and CEO of the Centre for Wildlife Studies in India, where she and her team work to protect a wide range of species, from Asian elephants and tigers to honey bees. She spoke to us on Perspective.

items.title: What's next for Iran after the funeral of their late supreme leader?
items.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent looks at how Iran's new leadership will respond to several issues affecting the country.

items.title: Trump refuses to sign US housing bill over voting act standoff
items.description: The housing legislation will become US law at midnight with or without President Donald Trump's signature.
